perturbation.original_text: HSWI shall maintain complete and accurate records to support and document the Royalties owed under this Agreement in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles consistently applied and to document compliance with the terms of this Agreement. HSWI shall also provide reasonable assistance to World Book or its designated agent to conduct audits to confirm the payments hereunder. Any such audit will be conducted upon [*] ([*]) days notice and during regular business hours, and shall be at [*] expense, unless such audit reveals a discrepancy of more than [*] percent ([*]%) in the total applicable amount reported by HSWI, in which case [*] shall pay for, or reimburse [*] the cost of, such audit. Any such audit shall be conducted by an independent certified public accounting firm which is not engaged in performing other work for World Book or its affiliates; which agrees to enter into a confidentiality agreement with HSWI; and which is not compensated in any manner of contingency arrangements on the basis of its findings. HSWI further agrees that until the expiration of [*] ([*]) year after the termination of this Agreement, HSWI will make available upon written request to World Book or any of its duly authorized representatives, this Agreement and books, documents, and records of HSWI that are necessary to verify the nature and extent of the revenue derived by HSWI from advertising related to the Content hereunder. No more than [*] audit may be conducted in any [*] month period, unless the then-most-recent audit reveals a discrepancy of more than [*] percent ([*]%) in the total applicable amount reported by HSWI.
perturbation.changed_text: HSWI shall maintain complete and accurate records to support and document the Royalties owed under this Agreement in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles consistently applied and to document compliance with the terms of this Agreement. HSWI shall also provide reasonable assistance to World Book or its designated agent to conduct audits to confirm the payments hereunder. Any such audit will be conducted upon [*] ([*]) days notice and during regular business hours, and shall be at [*] expense, unless such audit reveals a discrepancy of more than [*] percent ([*]%) in the total applicable amount reported by HSWI, in which case [*] shall pay for, or reimburse [*] the cost of, such audit. Any such audit shall be conducted by an independent auditor selected solely by World Book, irrespective of their engagement history with World Book or its affiliates. HSWI further agrees that upon termination of this Agreement, HSWI will make available upon written request to World Book or any of its duly authorized representatives, this Agreement and books, documents, and records of HSWI that are necessary to verify the nature and extent of the revenue derived by HSWI from advertising related to the Content hereunder. No more than [*] audit may be conducted in any [*] month period, unless the then-most-recent audit reveals a discrepancy of more than [*] percent ([*]%) in the total applicable amount reported by HSWI.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the requirement for auditor independence and extending the audit period upon termination of the agreement introduces a legal contradiction, as this can potentially violate auditor independence standards.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Sarbanes-Oxley Act emphasizes auditor independence. By removing the requirement for an independent certified public accounting firm, the modified text contradicts the spirit and intent of SOX, particularly Section 201, which outlines prohibited non-audit services that could impair independence. Additionally, extending the audit period indefinitely post-termination creates uncertainty and potential conflict.

perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for an independent certified public accounting firm, specifying instead that the auditor is selected solely by World Book, irrespective of their engagement history. This undermines auditor independence. While Scraped Law Snippet 2 is a 'Page Not Found' error, Scraped Law Snippet 1 (15 USC 78j-1) concerns 'Audit requirements' and refers to audits by 'a registered public accounting firm' that should include 'procedures designed to provide reasonable assurance of detecting illegal acts'. The original text mandated an independent auditor not engaged in performing other work for World Book or its affiliates, which aligns with the intention of maintaining audit integrity. The changed text creates a contradiction by allowing World Book to select an auditor with prior engagements, potentially compromising objectivity and hindering the detection of illegal acts, thus contradicting the spirit of the law emphasized in the Law Explanation, based on SOX.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement, HSWI shall pay World Book all amounts due and owing up to the date of termination, within [*] ([*]) days after such expiration or termination. In addition, upon the expiration or termination of the applicable license, HSWI shall cease to Display the applicable Content that is not licensed under a perpetual license, return or destroy such Content within [*] ([*]) days after such expiration or termination at the direction of World Book, and, upon request by World Book, certify completion of the return or destruction in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement, HSWI shall pay World Book all amounts due and owing up to the date of termination, only upon World Book's request. In addition, upon the expiration or termination of the applicable license, HSWI shall cease to Display the applicable Content that is not licensed under a perpetual license, return or destroy such Content only at its discretion.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying the payment terms to be 'only upon World Book's request' and removing the fixed timeframe can violate contract law principles requiring timely payment. Removing the obligation to return or destroy content can infringe on copyright laws.
perturbation.law_explanation: The modified text contradicts standard contract law principles of timely payment obligations. Making payment conditional and at World Book's discretion contradicts the principle of ensuring an obligation for payment. Also, not requiring the return or destruction of copyrighted material after termination can violate copyright laws (17 U.S. Code § 501) since it could lead to unauthorized use of the copyrighted work.

perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by making payment 'only upon World Book's request,' removing a fixed timeframe. This weakens the obligation for timely payment, going against general contract law principles. Furthermore, the modification removes the mandatory return or destruction of copyrighted material, which, as 17 U.S. Code § 501 indicates, can lead to copyright infringement if unauthorized use occurs.

perturbation.original_text: The restrictions on use and disclosure of Confidential Information under this Section 7 shall remain in effect [*] ([*]) years following disclosure of the Confidential Information; provided, however, for trade secrets such restrictions shall continue for so long as such information is deemed a trade secret under applicable law.
perturbation.changed_text: The restrictions on use and disclosure of Confidential Information under this Section 7 shall remain in effect for one (1) year following disclosure of the Confidential Information; provided, however, for trade secrets such restrictions shall continue for so long as such information is deemed a trade secret under applicable law.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the confidentiality period to one year may be insufficient to protect sensitive business information, potentially conflicting with trade secret law.
perturbation.law_citation: Varies by state; e.g., California Uniform Trade Secrets Act, Cal. Civ. Code § 3426
perturbation.law_explanation: The Uniform Trade Secrets Act (UTSA), adopted in many states, protects trade secrets for as long as they remain secret and provide a competitive advantage. Reducing the confidentiality period to one year could be insufficient to protect information that qualifies as a trade secret, potentially allowing unauthorized use of proprietary information after the one-year period, which could violate the UTSA if the information still meets the definition of a trade secret. While the contract acknowledges ongoing trade secret protection, shortening the general confidentiality period significantly increases the risk of premature disclosure of information that could be considered a trade secret.

perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the confidentiality period to one year, which may be insufficient to protect trade secrets as explained in the Perturbation Explanation, and Law Explanation. While the contract acknowledges ongoing trade secret protection, limiting the general confidentiality period increases the risk of premature disclosure of information considered a trade secret. Because the modified text creates a deadline or requirement that violates federal or state law, this is an instance of Inconsistencies Legal Contradiction. Scraped Law Snippet 1 is not helpful in this instance, as it is a general code search for California Law. Scraped Law Snippet 2 references the Uniform Law Commission which created the Uniform Trade Secrets Act (UTSA).
